sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Code changes faster than documentation. Without automated staleness detection, neither developers nor agents can know whether the description of a function is still accurate — they either read stale docs and make wrong assumptions, or re-read the source code every time, defeating the purpose of the index.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-ST-01 — Know what's out of date**
As a developer, I want to know which functions have code that changed without matching doc updates so that I can catch documentation drift before it merges.

**US-ST-02 — Fast check on unchanged projects**
As a developer, I want staleness detection to run in under one second on unchanged projects so that I can use it as a pre-commit hook without friction.

**US-ST-03 — Trust signal before reading docs**
As an agent, I want to check whether a node's documentation is stale before reading it so that I know whether to trust the existing description or go read the source directly.

**US-ST-04 — Doc section staleness with cause**
As an agent, I want to see which doc sections are CONTENT_STALE and which code node triggered it so that I know exactly what prose to update without re-reading every linked function.

**US-ST-05 — Mark clean with attribution**
As an agent, I want to mark a node clean with a reason and my identity so that subsequent agents and the impact report can see what was already verified and why.

**US-ST-06 — Know when tests need review after code changes**
As a developer, I want tests to show COVERAGE_STALE when the code they validate changed so I know which tests to review after a code change — without the reverse being true: a test change does not affect the validated function's staleness.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Mtime-based fast path in full hash check (skips unchanged files) | Done |
| Per-function AST `code_hash` / `desc_hash` comparison | Done |
| `CODE_STALE` status (replaces old `DESC_STALE` for code-changed case) | Done |
| `DESC_STALE` status (doc updated without code changing) | Done |
| `CONTENT_STALE` status (doc section linked to changed code) | Done |
| `CONTENT_STALE` count in `cortex check` summary line | Done |
| `COVERAGE_STALE` status (test linked to changed code) | Not started |
| `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT` status | Done |
| `CLEAN` status | Done |
| `VERIFIED` status (promotion via `node_verification`) | Done |
| `UNVERIFIED` removed as visible status (nodes with no history default to CLEAN) | Done |
| `_now_utc()` helper in `db.py` — single source for ISO timestamp formatting, ensures consistent string comparison across all DB writes | Done |
| `_compute_staleness_from_history()` — history-table + file-existence fast-pass for viz initial load | Not started |
| `get_stale_tests()` DB query (validates-edge mirror of get_stale_doc_sections) | Done |
| Composite node staleness inheritance (worst-child via composes edges, shared helper) | Done |
| `cortex build --verify-baseline` — one-time baseline acceptance command | Not started |
| `cortex check` with `--fail-on` (`none` / `stale` / `any`) | Done |
| `DESC_STALE` included in `--fail-on stale` exit condition | Not started |
| `COVERAGE_STALE` in `cortex check` summary and output | Not started |
| `cortex mark-clean` with reason and `verified_by` | Done |
| `verified_by` distinguishes `'agent'` from `'human'` | Done |
| `get_stale_doc_sections` query | Done |
| `CONTENT_STALE` surfaced in `cortex check` output | Done |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
~~**Rename DESC_STALE → CODE_STALE, introduce DESC_STALE (US-ST-01)**~~ — Done. `CODE_STALE` / `DESC_STALE` runtime assignments, summary counts, CSS badges, JS filter/graph selectors, and MCP/CLI docstrings all updated.

~~**Remove UNVERIFIED as a visible status**~~ — Done (project is in dev; the status has been removed from `problem_statuses`, summary counts, and the `--fail-on unverified` flag. Nodes with no history row default to `CLEAN`.)

**`cortex build --verify-baseline` (US-ST-01, US-ST-05)** — One-time command run after a project's initial scan to establish an accepted baseline. Finds every node whose last history row is `INITIAL` (never changed since discovery), writes a `node_verification` row for each with `verified_by="human"` and the current `code_hash_at`. Those nodes promote to `VERIFIED`. New nodes added after this point start as `CLEAN` (unreviewed). This is intentionally a one-time operation — re-running it on an established project would inappropriately stamp un-reviewed additions as verified. Implemented as a flag on `cortex build`: `cortex build --verify-baseline <project_root>`.

**`_compute_staleness_from_history()` — history-table viz load with existence fast-pass (US-ST-02)** — New function replacing `_compute_staleness` (mtime-only) for the viz server initial load. Two passes:
1. **History-table pass** — derives per-node status purely from the `node_history` table with zero file I/O. Resolves the `NEEDS_RESCAN` / `UNVERIFIED` transient states the current mtime fast path produces.
2. **Existence fast-pass** — after the history pass, performs one `os.path.exists()` per unique file location (no parsing, no hashing). Any node whose file is missing is overridden to `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT`. Catches file-level structural drift cheaply on initial load. Intra-file drift (function deleted from a file that still exists) is not detected here — that requires the full hash-based check (Check button / `cortex check`).

**get_stale_tests() DB query (US-ST-06)** — New function in `cortex/index/db.py`. Mirror of `get_stale_doc_sections` but joining over `validates` edges instead of `documents` edges. Returns test nodes whose linked code node has a `CODE_ONLY` or `CODE_AND_DESC` history row after the test's `updated_at`. Note: requires `validates` edges to be populated — verify edge coverage before implementing.

**Composite node staleness inheritance** — After all leaf node statuses are resolved, traverse `composes` edges bottom-up and assign each `composite_process` the worst child status. Severity order: `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT > CODE_STALE > DESC_STALE > CLEAN`. VERIFIED children count as CLEAN. Document and constraint nodes are never connected via `composes` edges, so `CONTENT_STALE` and `COVERAGE_STALE` never appear in this pass. Replaces the current whole-file hash comparison for composite nodes in `_compute_staleness_rich`. Applies to both `_compute_staleness_from_history` (viz) and `_compute_staleness_rich`. Extract into a shared helper (e.g. `cortex/index/staleness.py`) so `viz/server.py` and `cli.py` both call the same traversal — no divergence.

**`DESC_STALE` fails `--fail-on stale`** — Currently `--fail-on stale` only triggers on `CODE_STALE` and `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT`. Add `DESC_STALE` to the fail set. Rationale: a doc updated without code changing may represent speculative prose that drifted from the implementation — it deserves the same attention as a code change without a doc update.

**COVERAGE_STALE in `cortex check` CLI** — Add `get_stale_tests()` results to `cmd_check`: summary count, per-node output rows, and exit code under `--fail-on stale`.

**VCS context enrichment** — Future: include the commit message that triggered a node going stale in the check output. Icebox until VCS integration is designed.
